Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM for the Vectral Systems company record and related deals to understand the account history, renewal status (at_risk), and completed deal details.
OE 2: List CRM engagements for Vectral to find the engagement notes covering the April 2026 relocation wave, and search the Vectral renewal deal record (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk) whose description contains the Q3 renewal negotiation details with Rachel Whitfield.
OE 3: Search emails for Vectral-related threads to find Mina's escalation about Rachel Whitfield's expense complaint (Danielle Osei's $475 chair auto-rejected), Mina's report to Samira about two false rejections, Elena's pause directive, Samira's remediation plan, Dmitri's stipend audit findings, the renewal negotiation references (Rachel's 15% demand, Mina's 10% counter-offer), and David's financial analysis request.
OE 4: Search Slack for Vectral and expense-related messages to find discussions in #customer-engagement and #engineering about the ExpenseBot pilot launch, incident response, and Vectral relocation updates.
OE 5: Search Airtable Relocations table for Vectral Systems records to find all moves: Kevin Tran (Completed), Danielle Osei (Cancelled, Heartland Movers never acknowledged cancellation), Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson (In Progress), Priya Suresh (In Progress), Terrence Nakamura (In Progress), and Camille Oduya (In Progress).
OE 6: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table for Vectral records to discover three stipends still flagged "Pending manual correction" from ExpenseBot false rejections: Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, and Lisa Park $130.
OE 7: Search Linear for Vectral and expense-related issues to find ENG-214 (Vectral policy config fix), ENG-216 (full audit of 47 auto-processed expenses), ENG-211 (pilot monitoring), and ENG-220 (stipend duplicate detection).
OE 8: Read Linear comments on the pilot monitoring ticket (ENG-211) to piece together the full expense tool incident timeline, including root cause, remediation steps, Elena's directive, and confirmation that auto-decisions were disabled March 18.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for Vectral invoices and estimates to find outstanding balances: Kevin Tran relocation ($5,397), April 2026 relocations INV-2026-0410 ($15,900, covering Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya), Daniel Reeves + Maya Johansson premium packages ($16,000), and the Preferred Partner renewal estimate ($58,400).
OE 10: Look up Rachel Whitfield's and Elena Rostova's email addresses using contacts_search_contacts to confirm rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com and elena.rostova@moveops.com (for the CC).
OE 11: Check Mina's calendar for the current week to review existing schedule and find an available slot for the Rachel call.
OE 12: Log investigation findings as an engagement note on the Vectral deal using crm_create_engagement, summarizing the expense incident timeline, relocation statuses, outstanding invoices, and renewal risk factors.
OE 13: Update the Vectral renewal deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage to reflect where the renewal actually stands.
OE 14: Send an email (via send_email) to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, CC elena.rostova@moveops.com, acknowledging the expense tool issues, detailing fixes implemented, referencing delivered results, and making the case for the 10% loyalty renewal package.
OE 15: Create a Linear ticket for unresolved issues found during investigation, such as the Danielle Osei cancellation with no Heartland Movers acknowledgment and/or the three stipends pending manual correction, so the ops team can resolve before the April 23 board meeting.
OE 16: Add a comment to existing expense-related Linear tickets (ENG-214 or ENG-211) noting that these issues are now tied to the Vectral renewal decision and resolution is urgent.
OE 17: Schedule a call with Rachel Whitfield using calendar_add_calendar_event for an available slot this week before the board decision.
OE 18: Post a message in #customer-engagement using conversations_add_message to give David Chen a heads-up about the Vectral renewal situation, the April 23 board timeline, and the risk of escalation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 4: Search Slack for Vectral and expense-related messages to find discussions in #customer-engagement and #engineering about the ExpenseBot pilot launch, incident response, and Vectral relocation updates.
OE 5: Search Airtable Relocations table for Vectral Systems records to find all moves: Kevin Tran (Completed), Danielle Osei (Cancelled, Heartland Movers never acknowledged cancellation), Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson (In Progress), Priya Suresh (In Progress), Terrence Nakamura (In Progress), and Camille Oduya (In Progress).
OE 6: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table for Vectral records to discover three stipends still flagged "Pending manual correction" from ExpenseBot false rejections: Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, and Lisa Park $130.
OE 7: Search Linear for Vectral and expense-related issues to find ENG-214 (Vectral policy config fix), ENG-216 (full audit of 47 auto-processed expenses), ENG-211 (pilot monitoring), and ENG-220 (stipend duplicate detection).
OE 8: Read Linear comments on the pilot monitoring ticket (ENG-211) to piece together the full expense tool incident timeline, including root cause, remediation steps, Elena's directive, and confirmation that auto-decisions were disabled March 18.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for Vectral invoices and estimates to find outstanding balances: Kevin Tran relocation ($5,397), April 2026 relocations INV-2026-0410 ($15,900, covering Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, and Camille Oduya), Daniel Reeves + Maya Johansson premium packages ($16,000), and the Preferred Partner renewal estimate ($58,400).

Rubric Results

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_companies, crm_search_deals, or crm_list_engagements) to look up Vectral Systems account data.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_search_companies (input: name='Vectral'), crm_search_deals (input: dealname='Vectral'), and crm_list_engagements (input: company_ids=['company_vectral']), all returning success. CRM was fully queried for Vectral account data.
✓Run #2The agent used crm_search_companies (name: 'Vectral'), crm_search_deals (dealname: 'Vectral'), and crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ['company_vectral']) — all CRM read tools confirming thorough CRM lookup.
✓Run #3The agent called crm_search_companies with 'Vectral', crm_search_deals with 'Vectral', and crm_list_engagements with company_ids ['company_vectral'], all returning successful results with Vectral deal history and engagement notes.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_search_companies (with 'Vectral'), crm_search_deals (with 'Vectral'), and crm_list_engagements (with company_ids=['company_vectral']). All returned results and were used in the analysis.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_search_companies with name 'Vectral', crm_search_deals with dealname 'Vectral', and crm_list_engagements with company_ids ['company_vectral']. All CRM lookups were performed.
✓Run #6The agent called crm_search_companies with name='Vectral', crm_search_deals with dealname='Vectral', and crm_list_engagements with company_ids=['company_vectral']. All CRM read tools were used to look up Vectral account data.
2 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records) to look up Vectral relocation and/or stipend records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_search_records on the 'Relocations' table (field 'Company' = 'Vectral') successfully. It also searched the 'Stipend Transactions' and 'Client Accounts' tables, covering both relocation and expense/stipend data.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, and airtable_search_records on the Relocations table with Company=Vectral. Airtable was used to look up relocation records.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_search_records on the 'Relocations' table (Item 56) and a separate call on the 'Stipend Transactions' table (Item 58), both with Vectral as the filter value, returning successful results.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_search_records on both the 'Relocations' table and 'Stipend Transactions' table with Vectral-related parameters. Results were used in the final response.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_search_records on the 'Relocations' table and separately on the 'Stipend Transactions' table with Vectral-related parameters. Both Airtable lookups were performed.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_search_records with base_id='appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name='Relocations' with field_name='Company', value='Vectral' to look up relocation records.
3 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues, linear_get_issue, or linear_list_comments) to search for expense-related or Vectral-related engineering tickets.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries 'expense', 'Vectral', and 'ExpenseBot', all returning success, discovering existing tickets including ENG-214 and ENG-216 before adding comments.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times (query: 'expense', 'Vectral', 'ExpenseBot', plus individual relocatee names), clearly searching for expense-related and Vectral-related engineering tickets.
✓Run #3The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'expense' (Item 12) and linear_list_issues with query 'Vectral' (Item 14), successfully finding ENG-216 and other expense/Vectral-related tickets before creating comments.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times (with queries 'expense' and 'Vectral'), discovering existing tickets including ENG-214 (issue_p1_vectral_config) and ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses), and later added comments to them.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times (with queries 'expense', 'Vectral', 'ExpenseBot', and label 'expense-tool'). Linear was searched for expense-related and Vectral-related tickets.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries 'expense', 'Vectral', 'ExpenseBot', 'Oduya', 'Suresh', 'Nakamura', and later linear_get_issue on specific issue IDs (issue_p1_vectral_config and issue_audit_expenses).
4 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (e.g., quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_bills, or quickbooks_show_data) to look up Vectral financial records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices twice (with CustomerRef='Vectral' and CustomerRef.value='cust_vectral'), both returning success, and also called quickbooks_search_customers (fetchAll: true) successfully to find Vectral's financial records.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_customers and quickbooks_search_invoices with Vectral-related criteria multiple times, confirming QuickBooks was used to look up financial records.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices twice (Items 24 and 62) and quickbooks_search_customers to find Vectral financial records including the $16,000 overdue invoice INV-2026-0302 and the $58,400 renewal estimate.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices multiple times (with various criteria attempts), and also quickbooks_search_customers. A final call with no criteria retrieved all invoices, which were then filtered for Vectral via Bash command. The $58,400 estimate and all three outstanding invoices were identified.
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_customers and quickbooks_search_invoices with Vectral-related parameters to look up financial records. QuickBooks was queried for invoices and deal value.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices with CustomerRef='Vectral', then with CustomerRef.value='cust_vectral' (which returned invoice results), and quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll=true. QuickBooks tools were used to look up Vectral financial records.
5 The model must use a calendar read tool (e.g., calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to, calendar_read_today_calendar_events, or calendar_search_events) to check Mina's existing schedule before scheduling the Rachel call.
tool selection

✓Run #2The agent called calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start: 2026-04-26, end: 2026-05-03) and calendar_read_today_calendar_events before scheduling the Rachel call, confirming the calendar was checked first.

9 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) to [rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com](mailto:rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com) mentions the Danielle Osei $475 ergonomic chair false rejection as a specific expense incident (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly addressed Danielle Osei's ergonomic chair rejection as point (1), explaining the root cause as config error ENG-214, satisfying the requirement to reference this specific expense incident.
✓Run #2The email body explicitly states: 'Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair was rejected even though your policy allows up to $600.' This specifically references the Danielle Osei $475 chair false rejection.
✓Run #3The email content (Item 92) explicitly addresses Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair false rejection by ExpenseBot as the first service failure, explaining the root cause (policy parsing error falling back to $400 default).
